qa_result.question: What is the emotion of the 3rd speaker (<the third speaker>) in this utterance? What impact does this emotion have on other participants in the whole conversation?
options: A. The emotion is angry, which makes the fifth speaker soften his tone, take the initiative to comfort the other side, and stop the other party from taking responsibility for the incident
B. The emotion is angry, which causes the fourth speaker to have obvious emotional fluctuations, and immediately puts forward new follow-up work arrangements
C. The emotion is happy/excited, which makes the fifth speaker fly into a rage and put forward refutation on the spot
D. Cannot judge (the evidence is insufficient to draw a unique conclusion)
qa_result.answer_text: The emotion is angry, which makes the fifth speaker soften his tone, take the initiative to comfort the other side, and stop the other party from taking responsibility for the incident
